name: Venezuelan Peppers with Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Seed and cut the bell peppers into thin julienne strips.
2. Peel and chop the garlic cloves into 1/4-inch chunks.
3. Openly slit the shrimp along their backs using a sharp knife; do not remove the vein but keep the shrimp intact. Set aside.
4. In a deep, heavy skillet or sauté pan, combine 2 cups of olive oil with the red bell pepper strips. Heat slowly until the peppers are extremely tender and no longer browned (the oil will turn a lovely rose color). Remove the peppers and set them aside.
5. Pour the remaining oil into another skillet and add the garlic pieces. Cook gently for 4 to 5 minutes, or until the garlic is tender and golden. Using a strainer or slotted spoon, transfer the garlic to the peppers.
6. Heat the garlic-infused oil until it shimmers slightly.
7. Add the minced chili pepper and shrimp to the skillet. Toss the shrimp in the oil until they are pink and cooked through.
8. To serve, arrange the peppers and their oil in a serving dish. Surround with shrimp. Each diner can soak bread slices in the oil and pile them high with peppers and garlic. Optionally, feta cheese may be added alongside the shrimp.
